result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-counters [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  monitor  Start monitoring a .NET application
  collect  Monitor counters in a .NET application and export the result into a file
  list     Display a list of counter names and descriptions, grouped by provider.
  ps       Lists the dotnet processes that can be monitored.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-counters [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  monitor  Start monitoring a .NET application
  collect  Monitor counters in a .NET application and export the result into a file
  list     Display a list of counter names and descriptions, grouped by provider.
  ps       Lists the dotnet processes that can be monitored.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Monitor counters in a .NET application and export the result into a file

Usage:
  dotnet-counters collect [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --counters                  A comma-separated list of counter providers. Counter providers can be specified as <provider_name> or <provider_name>[comma_separated_counter_names]. If the provider_name is used without qualifying counter_names then all counters will be shown. For example "System.Runtime[dotnet.assembly.count,dotnet.gc.pause.time],Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting" includes the dotnet.assembly.count and dotnet.gc.pause.time counters from the System.Runtime provider and all the counters from the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting provider. Provider names can either refer to the name of a Meter for the System.Diagnostics.Metrics API or the name of an EventSource for the EventCounters API. If the monitored application has both a Meter and an EventSource with the same name, the Meter is automatically preferred. Use the prefix 'EventCounters\' in front of a provider name to only show the EventCounters. To discover well-known provider and counter names, please visit https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/diagnostics/built-in-metrics.
  -p, --process-id            The process id that will be monitored.
  --refresh-interval          The number of seconds to delay between updating the displayed counters. [default: 1]
  --format <csv|json>         The format of exported counter data. [default: csv]
  -o, --output                The output file name. [default: counter]
  -n, --name                  The name of the process that will be monitored.
  --diagnostic-port, --dport  The path to diagnostic port to be used.
  --resume-runtime            Resume runtime once session has been initialized, defaults to true. Disable resume of runtime using --resume-runtime:false [default: True]
  --maxHistograms             The maximum number of histograms that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, histogram name, and dimension values counts as one histogram. Tracking more histograms uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 10]
  --maxTimeSeries             The maximum number of time series that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, metric name, and dimension values counts as one time series. Tracking more time series uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 1000]
  --duration                  When specified, will run for the given timespan and then automatically stop. Provided in the form of dd:hh:mm:ss.
  --dsrouter                  The dsrouter command to start. Value should be one of ios, ios-sim, android, android-emu. Run `dotnet-dsrouter -h` for more information.
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Monitor counters in a .NET application and export the result into a file

Usage:
  dotnet-counters collect [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --counters                  A comma-separated list of counter providers. Counter providers can be specified as <provider_name> or <provider_name>[comma_separated_counter_names]. If the provider_name is used without qualifying counter_names then all counters will be shown. For example "System.Runtime[dotnet.assembly.count,dotnet.gc.pause.time],Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting" includes the dotnet.assembly.count and dotnet.gc.pause.time counters from the System.Runtime provider and all the counters from the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting provider. Provider names can either refer to the name of a Meter for the System.Diagnostics.Metrics API or the name of an EventSource for the EventCounters API. If the monitored application has both a Meter and an EventSource with the same name, the Meter is automatically preferred. Use the prefix 'EventCounters\' in front of a provider name to only show the EventCounters. To discover well-known provider and counter names, please visit https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/diagnostics/built-in-metrics.
  -p, --process-id            The process id that will be monitored.
  --refresh-interval          The number of seconds to delay between updating the displayed counters. [default: 1]
  --format <csv|json>         The format of exported counter data. [default: csv]
  -o, --output                The output file name. [default: counter]
  -n, --name                  The name of the process that will be monitored.
  --diagnostic-port, --dport  The path to diagnostic port to be used.
  --resume-runtime            Resume runtime once session has been initialized, defaults to true. Disable resume of runtime using --resume-runtime:false [default: True]
  --maxHistograms             The maximum number of histograms that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, histogram name, and dimension values counts as one histogram. Tracking more histograms uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 10]
  --maxTimeSeries             The maximum number of time series that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, metric name, and dimension values counts as one time series. Tracking more time series uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 1000]
  --duration                  When specified, will run for the given timespan and then automatically stop. Provided in the form of dd:hh:mm:ss.
  --dsrouter                  The dsrouter command to start. Value should be one of ios, ios-sim, android, android-emu. Run `dotnet-dsrouter -h` for more information.
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display a list of counter names and descriptions, grouped by provider.

Usage:
  dotnet-counters list [options]

Options:
  -r, --runtime-version  Version of runtime. Supported runtime version: 3.0, 3.1, 5.0, 6.0, 7.0, 8.0 [default: 6.0]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Start monitoring a .NET application

Usage:
  dotnet-counters monitor [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --counters                  A comma-separated list of counter providers. Counter providers can be specified as <provider_name> or <provider_name>[comma_separated_counter_names]. If the provider_name is used without qualifying counter_names then all counters will be shown. For example "System.Runtime[dotnet.assembly.count,dotnet.gc.pause.time],Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting" includes the dotnet.assembly.count and dotnet.gc.pause.time counters from the System.Runtime provider and all the counters from the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting provider. Provider names can either refer to the name of a Meter for the System.Diagnostics.Metrics API or the name of an EventSource for the EventCounters API. If the monitored application has both a Meter and an EventSource with the same name, the Meter is automatically preferred. Use the prefix 'EventCounters\' in front of a provider name to only show the EventCounters. To discover well-known provider and counter names, please visit https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/diagnostics/built-in-metrics.
  -p, --process-id            The process id that will be monitored.
  --refresh-interval          The number of seconds to delay between updating the displayed counters. [default: 1]
  -n, --name                  The name of the process that will be monitored.
  --diagnostic-port, --dport  The path to diagnostic port to be used.
  --resume-runtime            Resume runtime once session has been initialized, defaults to true. Disable resume of runtime using --resume-runtime:false [default: True]
  --maxHistograms             The maximum number of histograms that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, histogram name, and dimension values counts as one histogram. Tracking more histograms uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 10]
  --maxTimeSeries             The maximum number of time series that can be tracked. Each unique combination of provider name, metric name, and dimension values counts as one time series. Tracking more time series uses more memory in the target process so this bound guards against unintentional high memory use. [default: 1000]
  --duration                  When specified, will run for the given timespan and then automatically stop. Provided in the form of dd:hh:mm:ss.
  --showDeltas                Shows an extra column in the metrics table that displays the delta between the previous metric value and the most recent value. This is useful to monitor the rate of change for a metric.
  --dsrouter                  The dsrouter command to start. Value should be one of ios, ios-sim, android, android-emu. Run `dotnet-dsrouter -h` for more information.
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.
